Thank you Casey for acknowledging this.

images.alt:
QUOTE from Casey Newton
"On the other hand, Telegram really does seem to be actively enabling a staggering amount of abuse. And while it’s disturbing to see state power used indiscriminately to snoop on private conversations, it’s equally disturbing to see a private company declare itself to be above the law.

Given its behavior, a legal intervention into Telegram’s business practices was inevitable. But the end of private conversation, and end-to-end encryption, need not be. Fending off onerous speech regulations and overzealous prosecutors requires that platform builders act responsibly. Telegram never even pretended to."

images.alt:
A worrisome outcome of France’s ultimate prosecution of Telegram, assuming there is one, is that it will embolden countries around the world to prosecute platform CEOs criminally for failing to turn over user data. We have already come worryingly close to this reality, and I have covered it closely here over the years. India and Russia were among the first countries to use so-called “hostage-taking laws” to threaten platform employees with jail over content moderation decisions, but many more have followed since.

images.alt: First-person perspective from floor level inside a vast ceremonial fox-temple. A fox-eared priestess stands directly over the viewer, looking down with one eyebrow subtly raised and a calm, knowing expression that suggests equal parts patience, amusement, and inevitability. Her hands rest firmly on her hips, posture strong and balanced, conveying authority without hostility. She wears flowing dark crimson and gold ritual garments adorned with delicate chains and filigree accents. Long black hair falls around her shoulders, catching shafts of warm golden light descending from above. Her stance is grounded and confident, one leg slightly forward, creating the impression that the viewer has just ended up exactly where she expected them to. The camera angle emphasizes her height and presence, while the background remains softly blurred: towering banners, warm torchlight, hints of temple architecture, and faint fox motifs. The focus remains entirely on the priestess herself—her gaze, posture, and expression communicating a silent, almost compassionate: "Well... what did you think was going to happen?"

images.alt: A dark-toned abstract realist painting with expressionist influences. Thick, gestural brushstrokes and layered textures create a sculptural sense of depth and movement. Diffused cool lighting and soft shadows add atmosphere and volume. Centered composition featuring a full-body figure seated atop a massive red mech, facing the viewer. The figure wears an intricate white-and-grey futuristic spacesuit with exposed mechanical components, utility gadgets, and head-mounted goggles. The environment is an alien landscape filled with drifting mist, oversized spores, moss-covered terrain, and unfamiliar plant life. The mech has a rugged industrial design with exposed pipes, bolts, weathered metal surfaces, and a transparent cockpit canopy. Muted colors with subtle tonal variation, cinematic mood, painterly texture, abstract realism, high detail.

record.bridgyOriginalText:
For work tasks, this matters a lot. Teams that draft emails, build reports, or plan campaigns using the same prompt template get predictable results. The trick is being consistent.

So before you blame the AI, check if your prompt changed even slightly between runs. Small wording changes lead to big output differences. Give it the same prompt 3 times. You'll probably see more consistency than you expected.

images.alt: Coq au vin is a classic French dish that translates to "rooster in wine". It is a stew made with chicken (often a tough rooster or hen), mushrooms, onions, bacon, and red wine, traditionally a Burgundy wine. The dish is usually prepared by first browning the chicken in a pot, then cooking it slowly in the wine and other ingredients until it is tender and flavorful. The red wine used in coq au vin gives the dish a rich, earthy flavor and a deep, reddish-brown color. Coq au vin is often served with potatoes or crusty bread, and is a popular dish in French cuisine.

The setting is the deck of a decrepit, cursed schooner called the Antelope, tossing on a violent, storm-tossed Atlantic ocean under a bruised, purple-black sky. I am standing at the helm, clutching the ship’s wheel with white-knuckled intensity. I’m dressed in tattered 1778 sailor garb—a rough wool coat, a loose linen shirt, and a red bandana tied around my head. My face is smudged with soot and sea spray, and my expression is a mix of terror and grim determination as I stare down a massive rogue wave. The rigging is tangled and snapping, and the sails are tattered shreds. The texture is gritty, with deep cross-hatching and heavy shadows to emphasize the chaos of the sea. The vibe is historical, desperate, and hauntingly epic.
